result:
Confirmed: `mm_update_next_owner` starting at line 446 encloses lines 521 & 527.

## `put_task_struct()` call sites in `kernel/exit.c`

Total: **6**

| # | file:line | enclosing function |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `kernel/exit.c:226` | `delayed_put_task_struct()` |
| 2 | `kernel/exit.c:521` | `mm_update_next_owner()` |
| 3 | `kernel/exit.c:527` | `mm_update_next_owner()` |
| 4 | `kernel/exit.c:1117` | `wait_task_zombie()` |
| 5 | `kernel/exit.c:1310` | (unnamed static helper ending at line 1323, called from `wait_task_stopped`/similar — enclosing function starts before line 1290; contains the `WSTOPPED` reporting logic) |
| 6 | `kernel/exit.c:1360` | `wait_task_continued()` |

(Note: `put_task_struct_rcu_user()` at line 282 is a distinct wrapper function, not a direct `put_task_struct()` call, so it's excluded from the count.)

## One complete acquire/release pairing

Site chosen: **`kernel/exit.c:521` and `kernel/exit.c:527`, inside `mm_update_next_owner()`**

Acquisition — `kernel/exit.c:508`:
```c
506: assign_new_owner:
507: 	BUG_ON(c == p);
508: 	get_task_struct(c);
```

Releases:
```c
519: 	if (c->mm != mm) {
520: 		task_unlock(c);
521: 		put_task_struct(c);   // release #1: mm changed under us, retry
522: 		goto retry;
523: 	}
524: 	WRITE_ONCE(mm->owner, c);
525: 	lru_gen_migrate_mm(mm);
526: 	task_unlock(c);
527: 	put_task_struct(c);   // release #2: normal fall-through after mm->owner assigned
528: }
```

Evidence this is a matched pair: `get_task_struct(c)` at line 508 takes an extra reference on `c` specifically so it can safely be examined/locked after `read_unlock(&tasklist_lock)` is called at line 518 (dropping the lock that was otherwise protecting `c` from disappearing). Control flow after that acquisition has exactly two exits from the block — the early-exit branch at line 519-522 when `c->mm != mm` (races with another mm change), and the fall-through path at line 524-527 after committing `c` as the new `mm->owner`. Each of those two exits calls `put_task_struct(c)` exactly once (lines 521 and 527), so every path following the line-508 acquisition drops the reference exactly once — a balanced acquire/release pair for the same object `c`.
